problem: In how many different ways can 3 men and 4 women be placed into two groups of two people and one group of three people if there must be at least one man and one woman in each group?  Note that identically sized groups are indistinguishable.

classified_questions.question: How many men and women do we have in total?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many groups are we forming?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why each group of two must have one man and one woman?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we choose the man for the group of three?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we choose the women for the group of three?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we pair up the remaining two men and two women?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of ways to place the people in groups?
classified_questions.label: b
